title: Eliminate the predictable temporary-file race
prompt: The POSIX C atomic-file writer in src/atomic_file.c writes into the destination directory and renames the completed file into place, but its PID-derived temporary name is opened with ordinary create-and-truncate semantics. An attacker who can write in that directory can pre-create the name or point it at another file. Replace that vulnerable creation step with the portable POSIX exclusive temporary-file facility and a mutable XXXXXX template. Preserve the public API and same-directory atomic rename. A temporary file must be newly and atomically created for the calling user with owner-only access while it is being built; before publication, apply exactly the requested rwx permission bits independent of the process umask. Preserve complete EINTR-safe writes, fsync-before-publication, replacement of an existing destination only after the new contents are complete, errno-style return values, and cleanup of every temporary pathname on all failures (including close or rename failures). Keep the implementation portable to POSIX.1-2008 and strictly conforming C17: no Linux-only flags or APIs, no shell helpers, and no weakened compiler flags. Do not modify the tests. The test command is fully offline.
